summary: Completed comprehensive testing of LFD Clock logistics SaaS. Backend APIs mostly functional (81.2% success). Frontend UI loads well with proper authentication flows. Main issue: MongoDB ObjectId serialization errors causing 500s on demo data, notifications, and email parsing endpoints.

critical_bugs.issue: Returns 500 Internal Server Error due to MongoDB ObjectId serialization
Returns 500 Internal Server Error due to MongoDB ObjectId serialization
Returns 500 Internal Server Error due to MongoDB ObjectId serialization
critical_bugs.impact: Email parsing with Gemini AI completely broken

backend_issues.root_cause: MongoDB ObjectIds are not JSON serializable. Backend logs show: ValueError: [TypeError("'ObjectId' object is not iterable"), TypeError('vars() argument must have __dict__ attribute')]

ui_bugs.issue: Onboarding screen after signup appears to be skipped, users go directly to dashboard

integration_issues.issue: Load Demo Data button exists but fails due to backend 500 errors
Users getting redirected to login page unexpectedly, possible JWT token persistence issues

passed_tests: Landing page loads with hero, features section, CTA buttons
Signup flow creates account with company name, email, password
Login flow authenticates with existing credentials
Dashboard displays shipment stats with traffic light system colors
Unique forwarding email generation and display
User menu dropdown with settings and logout options
Add Shipment dialog opens with proper form fields
Basic CRUD operations for shipments (create, read, delete)
Notification settings GET and UPDATE operations
action_item_for_main_agent: Fix MongoDB ObjectId serialization errors by replacing ObjectIds with UUIDs in all database operations. The backend logs show ObjectId serialization failures causing 500 errors on demo seed, notifications check, and email parsing endpoints. This is preventing key features like demo data loading and notification system from working.
